lede: A look at September 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Louis XIV and Alexandre Geijo, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Western Australia
events.desc: Western Australia becomes the last Australian state to abolish capital punishment.

events.title: Luxembourg
events.desc: Belgium, Netherlands and Luxembourg constitute Benelux.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japanese high command orders withdrawal at Milne Bay, the first major Japanese defeat in land warfare during the Pacific War.

events.title: Chile
events.desc: Chile: A group of youths affiliated with the fascist National Socialist Movement of Chile are executed after surrendering during a failed coup.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: First Battle of the Marne begins. Northeast of Paris, the French attack and defeat German forces who are advancing on the capital.

events.title: Exeter Theatre Royal fire
events.desc: A fire at the Theatre Royal, Exeter kills 186, making it the UK's deadliest ever building fire.

events.title: Louis XVIII
events.desc: Louis XVIII of France has to dissolve the Chambre introuvable ("Unobtainable Chamber").

events.title: Swa Saw Ke
events.desc: Swa Saw Ke becomes king of Ava

events.title: Liu Yan (emperor)
events.desc: Liu Yan declares himself emperor, establishing the Southern Han state in southern China, at his capital of Panyu.

births.name: Otto Bauer
births.line: Austrian philosopher and politician, Foreign Minister of Austria

births.name: Werner Herzog
births.line: German actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

deaths.text: Mother Teresa, Albanian-Indian nun, missionary, and saint, Nobel Prize laureate
Manuel Blanco Encalada, Chilean admiral and politician, 1st President of Chile
Emperor Shang of Tang, emperor of the Tang Dynasty

observances.text: Christian feast day: Zechariah and Elisabeth (Anglican and Eastern Orthodox Church)
